title: Final gate: batched Gemini review + scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs + tag v2.0.0-rc.3
scope: scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs (NEW), batched code review across all rc.3 commits, annotated git tag v2.0.0-rc.3
description: Single end-of-cycle gate per user MEMORY.md preference (batch review at end, not per-task). Three steps: (1) Copy scripts/rc2-coverage-gate.mjs to scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs with rc.3 ALLOWLIST; run it and address any per-file <90% gap by adding targeted tests. (2) Run batched Gemini review across all rc.3 commits (TASK-001 through TASK-008) using `ccw cli` or equivalent; resolve all Critical/High issues with follow-up commits. (3) After all gates green, create local annotated tag v2.0.0-rc.3 (NO push per convergence requirement).

files.change: Copy scripts/rc2-coverage-gate.mjs verbatim; replace ALLOWLIST with rc.3 net-new files: ['packages/server/src/services/review.ts', 'packages/server/src/tools/review.ts']. Replace DIFF_SCOPE with rc.3-extended files: ['packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts', 'packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts', 'packages/cli/templates/hooks/archive-hint.cjs (note: still excluded from coverage glob — comment retained)']. Update header comment 'rc.3 per-file coverage gate'.

implementation: Step 1.a: Copy scripts/rc2-coverage-gate.mjs to scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs; update ALLOWLIST + DIFF_SCOPE arrays + header comment
Step 1.b: Run `pnpm -r test:coverage` to generate coverage-summary.json for both server and cli packages

Step 1.c: Run `node scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs`; if exit 1, identify failing file(s); add targeted tests to bring per-file metrics >=90 lines/statements/functions and >=80 branches; re-run until exit 0
Step 2.a: List all rc.3 commits via `git log --oneline rc.2-tag-or-baseline..HEAD`; expect 8 commits (TASK-001 through TASK-008)

Step 2.b: Run batched Gemini review: `ccw cli -p 'Review the rc.3 implementation across all commits since v2.0.0-rc.2 tag. Focus on: correctness of fab_review service logic (especially layer-flip id reallocation + git mv across layer roots), 2-phase event emission completeness, doctor synthesized event reason convention, hook signal precedence, install pipeline regression. Surface Critical/High issues only.' --tool gemini --mode analysis --context '@packages/server/src/services/review.ts @packages/server/src/tools/review.ts @packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts @packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-review/SKILL.md @packages/cli/templates/hooks/archive-hint.cjs @packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts @packages/server/__tests__/integration/fab-review.test.ts'`
Step 2.c: Parse Gemini output; resolve all Critical/High issues with follow-up commits (style 'fix(rc.3): <issue>'); ignore Low/Info issues for rc.3 (defer to rc.4)
Step 2.d: Re-run Gemini review until zero Critical/High remain

Step 3.a: Verify all acceptance criteria 1-12 from handoff.json (read each, tick off): fab_review actions present (1), batch ids accepted (2), 2-phase events for promote (3), layer flip with id reallocation (4), search filters (5), skill mode inference (6), AskUserQuestion locked to genuine choices (7), skill installed at both paths (8), hook second signal (9), filesystem-edit fallback (10), dogfood evidence in events.jsonl (11), per-file coverage >=90% (12)
Step 3.b: Verify all 8 commits are on main with conventional-commit subject lines: `git log --oneline | head -10` and confirm each is single-purpose atomic commit

Step 3.c: Create local annotated tag: `git tag -a v2.0.0-rc.3 -m 'fabric v2.0.0-rc.3: review/promote half — fab_review MCP + fabric-review skill + filesystem-edit fallback. See .workflow/.lite-plan/fabric-v2-rc3-impl-2026-05-10/dogfood-evidence.md for empirical evidence.'` — NO push (convergence requirement)

Step 4: Write final gate report (.workflow/.lite-plan/fabric-v2-rc3-impl-2026-05-10/code-review.md) summarizing: coverage gate results, Gemini review findings + resolutions, acceptance criteria checklist, tag confirmation. NO commit needed for this report (lives in workflow folder)

reference.pattern: Batched-end gate pattern (per user MEMORY.md feedback_review_batching)
reference.examples: Mirror rc.2 gate execution: coverage gate first, Gemini review second, tag last; resolve Critical/High before tag

criteria: scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs exists and exits 0 — confirms review.ts + tools/review.ts >=90% lines/statements/functions and >=80% branches
Batched Gemini review produces zero Critical/High open issues — confirmed in code-review.md
All 12 acceptance criteria from handoff.json verified — checklist in code-review.md
8 atomic conventional commits on main between rc.2 and rc.3 tag — verified by git log
Local annotated tag v2.0.0-rc.3 exists — verified by git tag -l v2.0.0-rc.3
Tag NOT pushed — verified by `git ls-remote origin --tags | grep -c v2.0.0-rc.3` returning 0
code-review.md gate report committed to workflow session folder OR exists locally (workflow folder; not commit-required)

rationale.chosen_approach: Three-phase end-of-cycle gate: coverage first (objective), Gemini review second (subjective but exhaustive), acceptance + tag third (formal closure)
alternatives_considered: Per-task Gemini review — rejected by user MEMORY.md (batch at end)
Push tag to remote during rc.3 — rejected by convergence requirement (NO push)
Skip batched Gemini and rely on integration tests + coverage gate alone — rejected: Gemini catches design-level issues (e.g. unclear API ergonomics) that tests do not
Run gate before TASK-008 dogfood — rejected: dogfood is empirical evidence that some gates depend on (e.g. acceptance criterion 11)
decision_factors: Acceptance criteria 11 + 12 require dogfood evidence + coverage parser, must run AFTER all artifacts land
rationale.tradeoffs: End-of-cycle gate cannot catch per-task drift early; mitigated by coverage gate ALLOWLIST being scoped to net-new files only (so existing-file regressions still surface in standard test runs per task)

manual_checks: After tag, verify `git describe` reports v2.0.0-rc.3
Verify scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs is a valid CLI script (chmod 0755 if needed; node scripts/rc3-coverage-gate.mjs runs cleanly)

risks.description: Batched Gemini surfaces a Critical issue requiring redesign (e.g. layer-flip semantics flaw) — would force rework across multiple tasks
risks.mitigation: Schemas are pre-locked, eliminating most design-level Gemini findings; service logic is closely modeled on extract-knowledge.ts which Gemini already reviewed in rc.2
risks.fallback: If Critical surfaces: open a follow-up rc.3.1 task; Gemini issue resolution itself can be batched in a single fix(rc.3.1) commit

risks.description: Per-file coverage on review.ts <90% due to error-path branches (git mv failure) being hard to exercise on Windows CI
risks.mitigation: TASK-007 includes describe.skipIf(win32) for chmod-based negative test; coverage gate's branches threshold is 80 (not 90) accounting for platform skips
risks.fallback: Add vi.spyOn-based mock test for the failure path that runs cross-platform; bumps line coverage without requiring chmod

key_functions.purpose: Per-file coverage gate; reads coverage-summary.json from server+cli; asserts ALLOWLIST files >=90/90/90/80 (lines/statements/functions/branches)
